=20
Microbial Genomics pioneers all areas of genome research span= ning the breadth of microbial life including viruses, bacteria, archaea and= microbial eukaryotes. We welcome articles showing novel insights, new appl= ications, or innovative approaches using genomic data. Content ranges from = comparative and functional genomics of model organisms to population-scale = evolution, epidemiology and microbiome studies, and real-world genomics app= lications with clinical, veterinary or environmental relevance. See full journal scope

For eve= rything you need to know when preparing a submission check out our new <= a href=3D"https://www.microbiologyresearch.org/upload/AuthorChecklists/auth= or.checklist.MGEN.pdf" target=3D"_blank">submission checklist. = ;
